description: A finance-master record defining a tax rate by type and jurisdiction, and whether the tax is recoverable (input-credit eligible). Built new because both companies held only GST/PAN as registration data; a real multi-country P2P (India GST/TDS, Oman 5% VAT/WHT, import reverse-charge, customs duty) needs tax as a first-class line attribute so the three-way match reconciles invoice amounts INCLUDING tax. A PO line, invoice line, and payment withholding each reference a tax code. Maintainable individually or by bulk import (natural-key upsert by code).

fields.name: code
fields.label: Tax Code
fields.validation: non-blank; uppercase; natural primary key; unique; immutable; the bulk-import upsert key for the tax-code master
fields.purpose: The stable code a PO line / invoice line / payment references to apply a tax rate; the natural key bulk import upserts on. Links a taxable amount to its rate and recoverability so the match can reconcile tax.

fields.name: type
fields.label: Tax Type
fields.validation: one of the TaxType enum {GST | VAT | duty | reverse-charge | withholding(TDS/WHT)}
fields.purpose: Classifies the tax so the tax service applies the right treatment: GST/VAT on lines, duty on imports, reverse-charge self-assessed on imports, withholding deducted at payment (TDS/WHT on cross-border services). The branch the tax engine reads.

fields.name: rate
fields.label: Rate %
fields.validation: >= 0; percent; e.g. 18 (India GST), 5 (Oman VAT), 2 (TDS); applied to the taxable amount
fields.purpose: The percentage applied to a line's taxable amount to compute taxAmount; the figure the three-way match uses to verify invoice tax equals expected tax within tolerance.

fields.name: jurisdiction
fields.label: Jurisdiction
fields.validation: country/state code or name, e.g. IN, OM, IN-KA; identifies the taxing authority
fields.purpose: The taxing authority the code belongs to so the same logical tax (e.g. VAT) can carry different rates per country and tax determination selects the right code for a supplier's address jurisdiction.

fields.name: recoverable
fields.label: Recoverable (Input Credit)
fields.validation: true if the tax is input-credit eligible (claimable against output tax); false for non-creditable taxes
fields.purpose: Flags whether the paid tax can be reclaimed as input VAT/GST credit; the tax service tracks recoverable tax separately so net tax cost reporting and credit reconciliation are correct.

fields.name: isReverseCharge
fields.label: Reverse Charge
fields.validation: true when the buyer self-assesses the tax (imports/cross-border); typically paired with type reverse-charge
fields.purpose: Marks a code where the buyer accounts for the tax instead of the supplier; the import reverse-charge mechanism the tax service applies so import lines self-assess VAT/GST without a supplier-charged amount.

fields.name: status
fields.label: Status
fields.validation: INACTIVE not selectable on new lines; existing lines retain their code; a superseded rate is retired by INACTIVE plus a new ACTIVE code
fields.purpose: Lets compliance retire a tax code when a rate changes without altering historical lines that used the old rate; the new rate is a new ACTIVE code, preserving the audit trail.

invariants: A8 the three-way/two-way match reconciles amounts INCLUDING tax; a tax-mismatch raises a typed exception routed to Tax/Compliance
A12 tax amounts normalize to base currency for thresholds/analytics
A13 audit + SSE on every committed master change
type drawn from the TaxType controlled vocabulary
